article_title: Editas Medicine's CRISPR-Cas12a Therapy Achieves Functional Cure in 27 of 28 Sickle Cell Patients as RUBY Trial Results Land in NEJM

content_preview.title: Editas Medicine's CRISPR-Cas12a Therapy Achieves Functional Cure in 27 of 28 Sickle Cell Patients as RUBY Trial Results Land in NEJM
content_preview.summary: The RUBY trial of renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel), a one-time CRISPR-Cas12a gene-editing therapy, showed that 27 of 28 patients with severe sickle cell disease had no painful crises after treatment, with hemoglobin levels normalizing to 13.8 g/dL by six months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A gene-editing therapy that modifies a patient's own blood-forming stem cells has produced what researchers are calling a functional cure for severe sickle cell disease. Results from the multicenter RUBY trial, [published in the New England Journal of Medicine on April 1, 2026](https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2415550), showed that 27 of 28 patients treated with renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel) experienced no painful vaso-occlusive crises after a single infusion o...
